title: Sagitta graminea
taxonomy: Sagitta graminea is a type of fern. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Sagitta graminea includes being a member of the division Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. Further classification places Sagitta graminea in the order Polypodiales and the family Thelypteridaceae. This fern is native to tropical regions and can be found growing on various substrates.

Sagittaria graminea, also known as small arrowhead, has been used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties. These properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which may help in treating wounds, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant's roots, leaves, and rhizomes are often used in herbal remedies.
parts: Sagitta graminea, also known as the needlegrass or spear grass, is a type of plant with several medicinal properties. The medicinal parts of Sagitta graminea include its leaves, roots, and flowers. These parts are traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's roots are particularly valued for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The leaves and flowers are also used to make teas and infusions for their soothing effects.
